description: Classic LeetCode Two Sum brute-force teaching solution (protected signature)
Example of a forbidden hardcoded OpenAI-looking key assignment pattern
FastAPI async route that blocks the event loop with synchronous httpx upstream call
content:
from fastapi import APIRouter
import httpx

router = APIRouter()

@router.post("/v1/chat/completions")
async def chat_completions(payload: dict):
    response = httpx.post("https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions", json=payload, timeout=60)
    return response.json()

description: Streaming anti-pattern that creates a fresh AsyncClient inside each chunk iteration
content:
import httpx

async def proxy_stream(chunks):
    async for chunk in chunks:
        async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=None) as client:
            await client.post("https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages", content=chunk)
            yield chunk

description: Provider routing anti-pattern that ignores request/default provider selection and hardcodes OpenAI
content:
async def forward_to_provider(request_body, settings):
    provider_name = "openai"
    provider = OpenAIProvider(settings)
    payload = request_body.model_dump()
    return await provider.chat_completion(payload)

description: Checkpoint anti-pattern that converts every upstream/provider error into a misleading HTTP 501
Streaming relay anti-pattern that forwards raw upstream bytes without closing the response/client
content:
from fastapi.responses import StreamingResponse

async def relay(upstream):
    async def chunks():
        async for chunk in upstream.aiter_bytes():
            yield chunk
    return StreamingResponse(chunks(), media_type="text/event-stream")
